runs.label: vllm-minimax-m27-ngram4-p512-n256
runs.error: ValueError: No available memory for the cache blocks
observations: vLLM accepted the n-gram speculative config.
vLLM disabled async scheduling for n-gram speculative decoding.
The extra speculative capture/KV budget pushed the run below the available-KV threshold.

runs.label: vllm-minimax-m27-ngram2-gpu095-p512-n256
observations: Increasing gpu_memory_utilization to 0.95 and reducing speculative depth to 2 allowed the run to initialize.
Output throughput fell to 12.27 tok/s versus non-speculative BF16 u4 p512/n256 results in the mid-30 tok/s range.
The random-token benchmark has poor n-gram match characteristics, and vLLM's n-gram path disables async scheduling on XPU.

conclusion: MiniMax n-gram speculation is not a general performance path for the current random-token benchmark. It may still be useful for repetitive real workloads, but only after VRAM headroom is improved or a workload-specific acceptance test shows enough accepted tokens to offset disabled async scheduling and speculative overhead.
